title: mentha longifolia var. dumortieri
intro: Mentha longifolia var. dumortieri, also known as white peppermint or mint of the Alps, is a variant of the long-leaved mint species, characterized by its distinctive white or pale yellow flowers and a sweet, minty fragrance. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia var. dumortieri include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce stress and improve mental clarity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and essential oil forms to harness its medicinal properties, and is also used as a flavoring agent in culinary and pharmaceutical applications.
